title: Enchiridion 1: What Is in Our Power
description: The opening of the Stoic handbook: the fundamental distinction between what depends on us and what does not.
introduction: The opening of the Stoic handbook: the fundamental distinction between what depends on us and what does not.

translation.original_text: τῶν ὄντων τὰ μέν ἐστιν ἐφ᾿ ἡμῖν, τὰ δὲ οὐκ ἐφ᾿ ἡμῖν. ἐφ᾿ ἡμῖν μὲν ὑπόληψις, ὁρμή, ὄρεξις, ἔκκλισις.
translation.text: Of things that exist, some are in our power and some are not. In our power are opinion, impulse, desire, and aversion.
